content.full_text:
RAvI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
January 9, 1997
Michael D. Ott
Executive Officer
San Diego LAFCO
1600 Pacific Highway, Room 452
San Diego, California 92101
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-96-341
Dear Mr. Ott:
This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the “Act”)’ on behalf of the San Diego Local Agency Formation Commission.
QUESTION
Do the changes to the “Campaign Contribution Disclosures Provisions/Party Disclosure
Form” made by the LAFCO in response to the approval of Proposition 208 comply with the Act
as amended by Proposition 208?
CONCLUSION
The draft amendments to the form are unnecessary because Proposition 208 did not
change the requirements of Section 84308.
FACTS
In response to the approval of Proposition 208, the LAFCO has drafted amendments to
its form, “Campaign Contribution Disclosures Provisions/Party Disclosure” (the “form”). You
have transmitted a draft of the amended form for our comments. (A copy of this draft amended
form is attached to this letter as Exhibit A.)
Government Code sections 81000- 91015. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18000-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No. A-96-341
Page 2
In its original version, the form warns commissioners ofthe applicability and
requirements of Section 84308, “Contributions to Members of Appointed Boards or
Commissions; Disqualification,” and Regulation 18438, on the same subject. In general terms,
these provisions forbid certain officials from accepting, soliciting, or directing a contribution of
more than $250 from certain persons with business before the agency while that business is
pending before the agency and for a certain time afterward.
The draft amendments attempt to adapt the form to the contribution limits imposed by
Proposition 208. (See Section 85301 et seq., as effective January 1, 1997.) Specifically, the draft
attempts to reconcile the $250 contribution limit imposed by Section 84308 with the new
contribution limits under Proposition 208.
ANALYSIS
The commissioners of the LAFCO are appointed to their positions, and therefore are
subject to Section 84308.
Proposition 208’s contribution limits are keyed to candidates and elections. Proposition
208’s fundamental limitations on contributions to candidates apply “... for each election in which
the candidate is attempting to be on the ballot or is a write-in candidate.” (Section 8530 1(a), (b),
(c), emphasis added.) Thus, if a LAFCO Commissioner is a candidate for an elective office, then
the contribution limits of Section 85301 et seq. apply to him/her with respect to that office.
However, nothing in Proposition 208 affects the limitations on contributions based on
Section 84308. The draft amendments to the form appear to be based upon the faulty assumption
that the contribution limits of Section 85301 et seq. apply generally to the commissioners based
upon the size of the jurisdictions they represent on the commission for purposes of proceedings
subject to Section 84308. The contribution limits of Section 85301 et seq. do not apply to the
commissioners on that basis alone. Thus, the draft amendments to the form are, as a general
matter, unnecessary because the contribution limits of Section 85301 et seq. do not change the
existing limit of Section 84308 and, therefore, need not be reconciled with the more specific
contribution limit of Section 84308.
However, please note that the contribution limits of Section 85301 et seq. would apply to
a LAFCO commissioner who is presently an elected official or a commissioner who runs for an
elective office while retaining his or her seat on the LAFCO. Under these circumstances, the
Prop. 208 contribution limitations and the existing contribution limitation in Section 84308
would bh be applicable, much like local contribution limits equal to or lower than the $250
amount in Section 84308 have functioned exclusively of one another.


File No. A-96-341
Page 3
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
G

By
John V4rgelli
Staff Cdunsel, Legal Division

QUESTION: Do the changes to the “Campaign Contribution Disclosures Provisions/Party Disclosure
Form” made by the LAFCO in response to the approval of Proposition 208 comply with the Act
as amended by Proposition 208?

CONCLUSION: The draft amendments to the form are unnecessary because Proposition 208 did not
change the requirements of Section 84308.
